key_insight: An ethanol-assisted interfacial modification enables non-optical-contact, clamp-free laser transmission welding of CFRP to fused silica, overcoming the usual difficulty of joining highly dissimilar, transparent/absorbing materials.
engineering_relevance: The approach could simplify precision joining in lightweight, multi-material assemblies by reducing fixture complexity and expanding laser welding to CFRP–glass/ceramic interfaces.
method: Modify the CFRP/fused-silica interface with ethanol before welding
Use laser transmission welding without optical contact or clamping
Exploit interfacial condition changes to improve energy coupling and joint formation
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal article describing the method, but it does not provide numerical performance metrics or comparative evaluation details.
watch_item: Practical adoption may depend on how consistently the ethanol treatment controls interfacial state and whether joint strength remains robust under process variability and service conditions.
paper_title: Non-optical-contact and clamp-free laser transmission welding of CFRP to fused silica via ethanol-assisted interfacial modification
